title: Semi-Automatic Reporting of Ordinary Surveys
text: saros (Semi-Automatic Reporting Ordinary Surveys) R package designed handle repeating surveys within project occur annually biannually. aims automate process summarizing reporting survey data, helping researchers save time maintain consistency across survey iterations. Specifically, SAROS produces highly customizable figures, text, analyses reports batch possible dependent-independent relations possible interest.

what: Connection to the Astronomical concept of Saros
title: Semi-Automatic Reporting of Ordinary Surveys
text: term “saros” also refers cyclical phenomenon astronomy known Saros cycle, represents period approximately 18 years, 11 days, 8 hours. one Saros, Sun, Earth, Moon return approximately relative positions, leading similar eclipse. Cyclical occurrences: versions SAROS refer cyclical events recur regular intervals. case R package, relates surveys repeated annually biannually, astronomy, refers recurring pattern eclipses. Consistency: SAROS R package aims maintain consistency reporting analysis across survey iterations, just astronomical Saros cycle represents predictable pattern occurrence solar lunar eclipses. Time-saving: SAROS R package designed automate streamline process summarizing reporting survey data, saving researchers time effort. Similarly, Saros cycle useful tool astronomers predict plan future eclipses, making easier study events without make complex calculations time.

Dynamic dots <dynamic-dots> Arguments forwarded corresponding functions create elements. path Output path scalar<character> // default: NULL (optional) Path save output. title Title report scalar<character> // default: NULL (optional) Added automatically YAML-header index.qmd-file. authors Authors entire report vector<character> // default: NULL (optional) NULL, infers chapter_overview$authors, collates entire report. mesos_report Whether produce reports per mesos group scalar<logical> // default: FALSE false, returns regular single report. mesos_var Variable ´data´ indicating groups tailor reports scalar<character> // default: NULL (optional) Column name data indicating groups mesos reports produced. label_separator Variable label separator scalar<character> // default: NULL (optional) String split labels main question sub-items. name_separator Variable name separator scalar<character> // default: NULL (optional) String split column names data main question sub-items report_yaml_file Path YAML-file insert index.qmd scalar<character> // default: NULL (optional) Path file used insert header YAML, report. chapter_yaml_file Path YAML-file insert chapter qmd-file scalar<character> // default: NULL (optional) Path file used insert header YAML, chapter. qmd_start_section_filepath, qmd_end_section_filepath Path qmd-bit start/end qmd scalar<character> // default: NULL (optional) Path qmd-snippet placed /body chapter qmds. index_filename Index filename scalar<character> // default: "index.qmd" (optional) name main index Quarto file (subfolder) used collect chapters. groupby Grouping columns vector<character> // default: NULL (optional) Column names used identifying chapters sections. element_names Elements reported vector<character> // default: NULL (optional) Elements reported sets (batteries) y-variables. sort_by sort output vector<character> // default: NULL (optional) Sort output (collapse requested). ".top"proportion highest category available variable. ".upper"sum proportions categories middle category. ".mid_upper"sum proportions categories including middle category. ".mid_lower"sum proportions categories including middle category. ".lower"sum proportions categories middle category. ".bottom"proportions lowest category available variable. ".variable_label"Sort variable labels. ".id"Sort variable names. ".by_group"groups argument. Character vector category labels sum together. data_label Data label scalar<character> // default: "proportion" (optional) One "proportion", "percentage", "percentage_bare", "count", "mean", "median". always_show_bi_for_by Always show bivariate -variable vector<character> // default: NULL (optional) Specific combinations -variable bivariates always shown. categories_treated_as_na NA categories vector<character> // default: NULL (optional) Categories treated NA. variables_always_at_top, variables_always_at_bottom Top/bottom variables vector<character> // default: NULL (optional) Column names data always placed top bottom figures/tables. return_raw USE scalar<integer> // default: FALSE Whether return raw static element. panel_tabset_mesos mesos panel tabset scalar<logical> // default: TRUE (optional) Whether mesos reports comparison group displayed Quarto panel tabset (TRUE), (FALSE). showNA Show/hide NA categorical variables scalar<logical> // default: NULL (optional) Whether show NA categorical variables (one c("ifany", "always", "never")). totals Include totals scalar<logical> // default: FALSE (optional) Whether include totals output. hide_label_if_prop_below Hide label threshold scalar<numeric> // default: NULL (optional) Whether hide label value. First missing   0,0,1,0,1, # First two missing   1,0,0,0,1, # One in middle missing   1,0,0,0,1, # All in the middle missing   1,0,1,0,0, # Last one missing   1,0,1,0,NA, # Last two missing   1,0,0,NA,NA, # Last three missing   NA,NA,NA,NA,NA # All missing ), nrow = 9, byrow = TRUE)), nm=stringi::stri_c(ignore_null=TRUE, "X", 1:5))  # What should be the output for person estimation according to Mislevy # Skipped=> 0, not_administered=>NA, all_missing=>NA y_p <- stats::setNames(as.data.frame(matrix(c(   1,0,1,0,1, # All present   0,0,1,0,1, # First missing   0,0,1,0,1, # First two missing   1,0,0,0,1, # Recoding for counting skipped, not_administered, all_missing, etc # Skipped=> 99, not_administered=>999, all_missing=>9999 y_info <- stats::setNames(as.data.frame(matrix(c(   1,0,1,0,1, # All present   99,0,1,0,1, #
